conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child, Alex, who is 19 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To determine Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to understand their current situation. Is Alex currently in full-time approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in full-time education. He's doing his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Could you tell me when Alex turned 19, or confirm whether Alex is under 20 years old? I need to check this to assess eligibility, as the age limits for Child Benefit depend on whether the young person is in education or training.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is under 20. He's 19 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just to make sure I have all the information I need - is Alex enrolled in approved education that is not higher education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications below degree level)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. He's doing A-levels, so it's not university or anything like that - just sixth form level qualifications.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying that.

One more important question: are you currently living in the UK, or do you otherwise meet the residence requirements (such as working in the UK while living abroad in certain circumstances)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information.

Just one final question to complete my assessment: Are you the person responsible for Alex? This means you're the one who Alex lives with and who is primarily responsible for their care.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex lives here at home with me and I'm the one looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Alex is 19 years old (under 20) and is in full-time approved education doing A-levels, which is non-advanced education below higher education level. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for young people aged 16-19 who are in full-time approved education or training. Since you live in the UK, are responsible for Alex, and Alex lives with you, all the eligibility criteria are met.

Please note that Child Benefit for young people in education typically stops when they turn 20, or when they leave approved education or training, whichever comes first.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and under 20. He is in full-time approved education (A-levels), which is non-advanced education below higher education level. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a young person aged 16-19 if they are in full-time approved education or training that is not higher education. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residence requirements, and is responsible for Alex who lives with them. Therefore, Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (19 years old), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. Alex is in full-time approved education (A-levels) and is under 20 years old. The claimant meets the residence requirements. Based on the rules that allow Child Benefit for young people aged 16-19 in approved education or training, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex is in full-time approved education (A-levels) and is under 20 years old. The claimant meets the residence requirements.
